summary: In-memory registry of Source instances, keyed by name with upsert semantics

requires: Source instances must return stable Name() values
guarantees: Add with duplicate name replaces the existing entry (upsert, not append)
List returns a defensive copy — mutations do not affect internal state
GetByName returns nil (not error) when not found

state: Holds []Source slice; not safe for concurrent use (no mutex)

observations.text: No mutex protection — Registry is not concurrent-safe. If multiple goroutines call Add/Remove/List simultaneously, data races will occur.
Remove uses slice-splice (append([:i], [i+1:]...)) which modifies the underlying array in place. Safe for single-goroutine use.

Name uniqueness is enforced across source types — two sources of different types cannot share the same name. This prevents aliasing but may be surprising when configuring multiple telegram channels with generic names.
